Name: neorv32_uart.clkgen_en_o
Description: Output that enables/gates the external clock generator feeding the UART; driven directly from uart enable control to start/stop UART clocking.
Justification: If clkgen_en_o is prevented from asserting or is driven incorrectly at integration, the prescaler/tick will not be provided and the watchdog counter will not progress, disabling the WDT functionality and preventing the module from performing its recovery/reset role (Denial-of-service on WDT).

Name: neorv32_uart.uart_txd_o
Description: Asynchronous serial TX output driven by the transmitter shift register to send serial bits to external devices.
Justification: If the TX line is forced, shorted, or otherwise made unavailable at integration, the module cannot deliver transmitted data, resulting in service loss. While it carries application data, the primary integration-level risk is preventing transmissions (DoS).

Name: neorv32_uart.uart_rxd_i
Description: Asynchronous serial RX input sampled and synchronized by the receiver FSM to capture incoming serial bytes.
Justification: An attacker who injects or tampers with uart_rxd_i can modify the received byte-stream and thereby corrupt data delivered to the system, changing program behavior or data content. The dominant concern is integrity of the incoming data flow.

Name: neorv32_uart.uart_rtsn_o
Description: Active-low RTS output indicating whether the UART is ready to receive; controlled by FIFO fill levels and hw flow-control enable.
Justification: If uart_rtsn_o is manipulated or driven to indicate 'not ready', a connected peer will stop sending data, preventing reception and causing a denial-of-service. Therefore this signal primarily impacts availability at integration.

Name: neorv32_uart.uart_ctsn_i
Description: Active-low CTS input sampled for transmitter enable decisions; prevents the transmitter from starting new frames when de-asserted and hw flow-control is enabled.
Justification: If uart_ctsn_i is forced to disallow transmissions, the UART transmitter will be prevented from sending, creating a DoS on outbound communication. The principal integration-level risk is loss of service.

Name: neorv32_uart.irq_rx_o
Description: Interrupt line asserted based on RX FIFO thresholds (not-empty, half-full, full) when enabled to notify CPU of receive events.
Justification: Suppressing or spurious assertion of irq_rx_o can prevent the CPU from being informed of received data (or flood it), effectively denying timely processing of incoming bytes. The dominant threat is interruption/denial of normal RX handling (availability).

Name: neorv32_uart.irq_tx_o
Description: Interrupt line asserted based on TX FIFO thresholds (empty, not-half-full) when enabled to notify CPU about transmit-space conditions.
Justification: Tampering with irq_tx_o can prevent the CPU from knowing when it can enqueue more data or can create spurious conditions, disrupting transmission scheduling. This primarily impacts availability of transmit service.

Name: neorv32_uart.ctrl.enable
Description: Control bit that globally enables or disables UART operation; gates transmitter, receiver and related logic and also drives clkgen_en_o.
Justification: Tampering with ctrl.enable (clearing or preventing it from being set) will stop the watchdog from starting/counting and thereby prevent the WDT from performing system recovery, causing a loss of the module's intended availability/safety function.

Name: neorv32_uart.ctrl.baud
Description: 10-bit baud divisor reloaded into per-bit counters to set the bit timing for transmitter and receiver.
Justification: If ctrl.baud is modified, the transmitter/receiver timing will change and cause corrupted communication (mismatched bit sampling), altering the correctness of exchanged bytes. This is a configuration that directly impacts data integrity.

Name: neorv32_uart.ctrl.prsc
Description: 3-bit prescaler selecting which bit of the clkgen_i vector is used as uart_clk (affects effective baud timing).
Justification: Tampering with the prescaler will alter uart_clk selection and thus change bit timing, which can corrupt transmissions and receptions. The primary risk is integrity degradation of serial data due to incorrect timing.

Name: neorv32_uart.rx_engine.over
Description: Status flag indicating an RX FIFO overrun (set when a write happens while fifo.free = '0'); exposed via control read status and used to detect lost incoming data.
Justification: rx_engine.over signals that incoming data was dropped due to a full FIFO; if suppressed, software cannot detect/recover from loss and the interface silently desynchronizes, while if stuck high it forces unnecessary clears/back-pressure, throttling throughput—both degrading availability.
